content:
- Executed task bug-test-harness-runtime-path-dependent-kill-command through to completion.
- Fixed remaining compile breaks after earlier lint/dead_code cleanup: updated HA e2e fixtures to stop depending on removed TestClusterHandle fields (build CliApiClient on demand; rebuild timeline locally; derive etcd proxy links by name prefix) and removed unused http_timeout_ms import.
- Removed dead `artifact_root` field from `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/config.rs` to satisfy `-D dead-code`.
- Verified the root fix: no `Command::new("kill")` remains under `src/`.
- Ran required gates: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint` all PASS.
- Updated task markdown: status/passes/passing tags set true + all checklist items ticked.
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed (`task finished ...`), and pushed to `master`.
- Added `.gitignore` entry for `target_verify_*/` and deleted stray `target_verify_*` dirs produced during runs.

- Note: This patch includes a broad dead_code cleanup to make `make lint` feasible under `-D warnings`. If any removed symbols were actually desired public API, re-introduce them with real use sites or `#[cfg(test)]` gating.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and applied bug-first rule.
- Found two bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>`: `bug-real-binary-provenance-enforcement-gaps.md` and `gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md`.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md` as the next task because it hardens the project’s verification gates (timeouts + fail-closed behavior) and reduces the risk of future false passes/hangs.
- Wrote that task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Investigated Makefile test partitioning and verified current ULTRA_LONG_TESTS wiring.
- Confirmed `make test` currently runs `cargo test --all-targets -- $(ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS)` with a timeout around execution only; preflight `-- --list` is unbounded.
- Confirmed `make test-long` preflights `-- --list` and runs each ultra-long test via `cargo test --all-targets "$t" -- --exact` but has no timeout wrappers.
- Verified all four ULTRA_LONG_TESTS names exactly map to real tests in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`; no `#[ignore]` segregation and no feature gate split.
- Ran `cargo test --all-targets -- --list` and counted totals: 271 total tests, 4 ultra-long, 267 non-ultra. This means default suite is currently meaningful, but there is no explicit Makefile guard for non-ultra count > 0.
- Key fail-open risk to fix: if all tests move into ULTRA_LONG_TESTS, `make test` can still return success without asserting non-ultra execution count.
- Next: provide parent with robust strategy: single preflight list file -> exact validation for ULTRA_LONG_TESTS -> compute and assert non-ultra set non-empty -> run default via explicit non-ultra list or validated skip path -> run ultra-long each with exact + timeout.

- Investigated docs no-code guard wiring and behavior for gate-audit bug context.
- Located hook in Makefile docs-lint target and read tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh.
- Found guard currently scans only hardcoded roots (architecture/concepts/interfaces/testing), which now resolves to 4 files because architecture/testing are empty after docs reorg.
- Found fence detection regex is /^```/ in awk (column-1 only), so indented fences are bypassed; also parser takes full info string via substr($0,4), so language+attributes are misparsed.
- Next useful step for main agent: implement root discovery from docs/src/SUMMARY.md or scan docs/src with explicit excludes (contributors/verification), and replace fence parser with whitespace-tolerant backtick+tilde matcher that tracks marker length and parses first info token.

- Inspected Makefile/test gates and docs-lint scripts for timeout + fail-closed behavior.
- Confirmed current gaps: unbounded `make check`, `make lint`, `make test` preflight list, `make test-long` preflight + per-test runs; docs no-code guard only scans 4 subtrees and only `^````, so whitespace-indented fences and moved docs directories can bypass.
- Drafted concrete fix proposal: add reusable gate-step wrapper (structured begin/end UTC, duration, command, exit status, timeout flag), wire explicit per-phase timeouts into all gate targets, and harden docs guard roots/patterns; include suggested timeout values.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md`.
- Audited current gate behavior in `Makefile`: `check`/`lint`/`test-long` and `test` preflight are unbounded; only `make test` execution is wrapped by `timeout`.
- Located docs architecture guard `tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh`: hardcoded roots miss most of `docs/src`, and it only matches column-1 ``` fences.
- Spawned parallel explorer subagents to analyze Makefile gates, docs guard bypasses, evidence logging conventions, and ultra-long test split risks.
- Wrote a detailed Draft 1 plan into `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md` and appended `TO BE VERIFIED` as required.
- Noted a minor shell footgun: an `rg` command containing ``` inside double quotes triggered command-substitution parse errors; need single quotes for regexes containing backticks.

- Next: follow the task protocol — when resuming, read plan under `TO BE VERIFIED`, do deep skeptical review (must change at least one thing), then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE` and implement: add `tools/gate-step.sh`, rewire Makefile targets with explicit per-phase timeouts + structured evidence, harden docs guard scanning + fence parsing, then run `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint` until 100% green.

- Read prior progress via `.ralph/progress_read.sh` and inspected current gate wiring in `Makefile` plus docs guard script.
- Confirmed actual timeout coverage is narrow: only `make test` final execution is wrapped by timeout; `test` preflight list, all of `test-long`, `check`, `lint`, and direct `docs-lint` are currently unbounded.
- Located Draft 1 plan in `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md` and compared proposed phases against real target bodies.
- Key insertion points identified: replace command bodies of `check`, `docs-lint`, `lint` clippy passes, `test` preflight/list/validate/exec, and `test-long` preflight/per-test loop with step-runner invocations.
- Found a concrete pitfall not explicit in Draft 1: Make uses `/bin/sh` by default (`make -pn` shows `SHELL = /bin/sh`) while recipes use bashisms (`set -o pipefail`, `[[ ... ]]`), so runner integration should set `SHELL := /bin/bash` (or avoid bashisms) to be portable.
- Proposed concrete Draft 1 upgrades for user response: make `docs-lint` itself timeout/evidence-backed, and centralize ultra-long preflight validation in a reusable helper script to avoid duplicate brittle shell blocks.

- Investigated docs guard + layout per request: read tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh, docs/book.toml, docs/src/SUMMARY.md, Makefile docs-lint, and bug plan .ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md.
- Quantified scope drift: guard scans only docs/src/{architecture,concepts,interfaces,testing}; current book has 44 markdown files with only 4 in scanned roots (~9.1% coverage). SUMMARY now centers on start-here/quick-start/operator/lifecycle/assurance/interfaces/contributors.
- Reproduced bypasses with temporary files under docs/src/interfaces and script execution:
  - leading-whitespace fenced rust block passes
  - ~~~rust fenced block passes
  - blockquote fenced rust block passes
  - raw HTML <pre><code class="language-rust"> passes
  - confirmed baseline plain ```rust fails as expected.
- Reproduced parser false-positive bug: ```bash title="ok" is rejected as disallowed lang due whole-info-string matching; also 4-backtick fences misparsed (````bash rejected).
- Drafted hardening direction: resolve docs src from book.toml, policy-map by path class (avoid global no-code over contributors), robust fence parser (indent <=3, backticks+tilde, first-token lang), detect html code tags, deterministic sorted scan, fail-closed for unclassified docs paths.
- Draft 1 improvement identified: change Phase 3 “scan all docs/src with one allowlist” into policy matrix + classification coverage check + regression fixture tests; make ~~~ support mandatory (not optional).

- Inspected .ralph conventions and current bug draft plan for gate hardening.
- Verified current Makefile behavior: only `make test` final execution is timeout-wrapped; preflight list, test-long, check, lint/docs-lint phases remain unbounded.
- Audited tooling layout: project gate/docs/install scripts live under `tools/`; Ralph workflow scripts and task protocol helpers live under `.ralph/`.
- Confirmed evidence gotcha: `.ralph/evidence/.gitignore` contains `*`, so evidence files are ignored unless force-added.
- Confirmed timeout environment: GNU `timeout` available; Makefile already resolves timeout/gtimeout and has ensure-timeout target.
- Confirmed docs no-code guard gaps: hardcoded roots and `^``` only.
- Prepared recommended Draft 1 changes: keep runner in `tools/` (or rename to `tools/make-gate-step.sh`), task-scoped evidence path, deterministic run-id with pid, explicit Makefile bash shell, docs-lint direct timeout coverage, and JSONL schema/escaping safeguards.

- Next: return concise plan-change proposal to user with file/line references and concrete edits to apply.
